channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
item.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.content:encoded: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
item.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
item.description: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded:
The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

(Image credit: Leon Neal)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
item.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

(Image credit: Vincent Thian/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
item.description: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.
item.content:encoded:
Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

(Image credit: Abbas Fakih)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump grants Iran another extension on a deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said he'll hold off on striking Iran's power plants until April 6, postponing a deadline for Iran to let ships transit safely through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said he'll hold off on striking Iran's power plants until April 6, postponing a deadline for Iran to let ships transit safely through the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Anadolu)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

(Image credit: Pamela Smith)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
item.description: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.
item.content:encoded: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng
